bugs.root_cause: DS-Coder's modeling_deepseek.py was written for transformers 4.x. The symbol `is_torch_fx_available` was removed in transformers 5.x. It is only used to wrap `_prepare_4d_causal_attention_mask` with `torch.fx.wrap` for FX tracing, which we don't use.
bugs.fix: Wrapped the import in try/except ImportError and provided a `def is_torch_fx_available(): return False` fallback. The downstream `if is_torch_fx_available():` block then no-ops.
bugs.notes: HF caches a copy of trust_remote_code modules under /srv/dev-disk-by-uuid-.../huggingface/modules/transformers_modules/<MODEL>/. After patching base/, you must `rm -rf` that cache dir or HF will keep loading the old copy and the patch will appear to do nothing.

bugs.error_message: RuntimeError: expected m1 and m2 to have the same dtype, but got: c10::BFloat16 != float
bugs.root_cause: RecurrenceCell (LTIInjection / IdentityBiasedGate / PerLoopLayerScale) was constructed in fp32 by default while the wrapped DS-Coder base loaded in bf16. When the wrapper called self.recurrence(h, e, block_out) at force_gate_zero=False, LTI's Linear B (fp32) hit bf16 e and PyTorch refused the matmul.
bugs.fix: After constructing self.recurrence, cast it to the base's dtype: `base_dtype = next(self.base.parameters()).dtype; self.recurrence.to(dtype=base_dtype)`. Note: phase 1+ will reintroduce fp32 explicitly inside the loop body around RMSNorm calls per MASTER_PLAN.md stability rule -- this dtype-match is only for the LTI Linear and gate/layerscale params themselves.
